name: Dropbox Sign API (HelloSign)
what_it_does: E-signature API (now Dropbox Sign) for embedding legally binding signature workflows, sending documents for signature, and managing signing status from agent workflows.
use_cases: Sending contracts for signature automatically from agent workflows
Embedding signature UI into web applications via embedded signing
Tracking document signing status and completion events via webhooks
Creating reusable signature templates for common document types
Bulk sending the same document to multiple signers
not_for: Very high-volume enterprise scenarios where DocuSign is the standard
Complex routing workflows requiring advanced conditional logic
Documents requiring notarization or advanced ID verification
best_when: You need a simpler, lower-cost e-signature API than DocuSign with clean developer experience and good SDK coverage.
avoid_when: Your customers explicitly require DocuSign for enterprise compliance, or you need advanced identity verification.

auth.notes: API key for direct server-side use. OAuth 2.0 for acting on behalf of users. API key is simpler for agent use. Basic auth with API key as username.

pricing.notes: Free tier allows testing but very limited. Essentials is reasonable for small volume. API access requires at least Essentials plan for production use.

performance.rate_limits: Not published; standard API throttling applies

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include error_name and error_msg fields. Validation errors describe the specific field issue. Webhook callback errors are logged in dashboard.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Signature request creation is not idempotent. Sending same document twice creates two separate signature requests.
known_agent_gotchas: Rebranded to Dropbox Sign - some documentation and SDK references still use HelloSign naming
Webhook events require endpoint verification via first-callback handshake before delivering events
Embedded signing requires generating a one-time sign URL that expires - agents must generate fresh URLs per signer session
Test mode signatures are legally non-binding - ensure PRODUCTION keys are used for real agreements
API key is tied to Dropbox Sign account owner - team members need OAuth apps for independent access

security_score_components.security_notes: API key and OAuth2. SOC2 Type II. Legal e-signature data — highly sensitive. Now called Dropbox Sign. ESIGN/UETA/eIDAS compliant. Audit trail for all signed documents.
